examples.title: Recursive DNS Query Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm showing how a recursive resolver processes a DNS query
examples.code:
FUNCTION ResolveRecursive(domainName, queryType)
 // Check local cache first
 cachedResult = CacheLookup(domainName, queryType)
 IF cachedResult exists AND NOT expired THEN
 RETURN cachedResult
 
 // Start resolution process
 serverList = GetRootServers()
 
 // Query root server
 response = QueryServer(serverList[0], domainName, queryType)
 
 WHILE response IS referral DO
 // Follow referrals up the chain
 nextServer = response.referralServer
 response = QueryServer(nextServer, domainName, queryType)
 END WHILE
 
 // Store in cache with TTL
 CacheStore(domainName, queryType, response, response.ttl)
 
 RETURN response
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: The recursive resolver handles the complete resolution by following referrals from root → TLD → authoritative servers. It checks cache first, then iteratively queries servers until it gets the final answer or determines the domain doesn't exist.

examples.title: Iterative DNS Query Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm showing how iterative DNS resolution works
examples.code:
FUNCTION ResolveIterative(domainName, queryType, dnsServer)
 currentServer = dnsServer
 
 FOR i = 1 TO maxRetries DO
 response = SendQuery(currentServer, domainName, queryType)
 
 IF response.status == NXDOMAIN THEN
 RETURN NULL // Domain does not exist
 ELSE IF response.status == SUCCESS THEN
 RETURN response.answers
 ELSE IF response.hasReferrals THEN
 // Switch to next server in chain
 currentServer = response.GetNextServer()
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN NULL // Resolution failed
END FUNCTION

// Example iterative resolution at resolver
FUNCTION ClientIterativeQuery(domainName)
 rootServers = [a.root-servers.net, b.root-servers.net, ...]
 
 // Ask root for .com TLD server
 tldServers = ResolveIterative(".com", NS, rootServers)
 
 // Ask .com TLD for authoritative server
 authServers = ResolveIterative("example.com", NS, tldServers)
 
 // Ask authoritative for A record
 ipAddress = ResolveIterative("example.com", A, authServers)
 
 RETURN ipAddress
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: In iterative mode, the resolver makes separate queries to each level. Each server responds with either the answer or a referral to the next server. The client (or resolver acting on client's behalf) handles following these referrals.
examples.timeComplexity: O(3) - exactly 3 queries typically (root, TLD, authoritative)

examples.title: DNS Cache Implementation
examples.description: Simple DNS cache with TTL expiration logic
examples.code:
CLASS DNSCache
 cache = {} // Map: (domain, type) -> {ip, expiryTime}
 
 FUNCTION Lookup(domain, recordType)
 key = (domain, recordType)
 entry = cache[key]
 
 IF entry == NULL THEN
 RETURN NULL // Cache miss
 END IF
 
 currentTime = GetCurrentTime()
 
 IF currentTime > entry.expiryTime THEN
 // Entry expired
 DELETE cache[key]
 RETURN NULL
 END IF
 
 RETURN entry.ip // Cache hit
 END FUNCTION
 
 FUNCTION Store(domain, recordType, ip, ttl)
 key = (domain, recordType)
 expiryTime = GetCurrentTime() + ttl
 
 cache[key] = {
 ip: ip,
 expiryTime: expiryTime,
 originalTTL: ttl
 }
 END FUNCTION
 
 FUNCTION Flush()
 // Clear entire cache
 cache = {}
 END FUNCTION
 
 FUNCTION Cleanup()
 // Remove expired entries
 currentTime = GetCurrentTime()
 FOR EACH key, entry IN cache DO
 IF currentTime > entry.expiryTime THEN
 DELETE cache[key]
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FUNCTION
END CLASS
examples.explanation: DNS caching reduces latency and server load by storing query results. Each entry has a TTL determining how long it remains valid. When TTL expires, the next query triggers a fresh lookup from authoritative servers.
examples.timeComplexity: Lookup: O(1), Store: O(1), Cleanup: O(n)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is cached entries
